nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: La
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Wez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ming, Wei, Li, Chao, Hong, Cheng, Jin, Ling, Ping, Yuan, Bin, Dong, Lan, Min, Chang, Chong, Chung, Houa, Pai, Sha, Yiming.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 魏, a term referring to ‘the buildings at either side of the gates of the royal palace’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Wei (魏), originally the name of a fief (located in Ruicheng in Shanxi province) granted to Bi Wan, an official in the state of Jin. In 403  BC , the state of Jin was split into three, one of which was the state of Wei (魏), established by Wei Huan Zi (魏桓子). After this state was annexed by the state of Qin in 225  BC , its name was adopted by some people as a surname. (ii) adopted in place of the surname Mi (羋) by Wei Ran (魏冉), an official who lived in the state of Qin during the Warring States period (475–221  BC ). (iii) borne by descendants of Wei Liao Weng (魏了翁), a scholar during the Southern Song dynasty (1127–1279  AD ), who changed his original name Gao (高) to his uncle's surname Wei (魏).

variants.name: Vi
meanings: Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 韋, see   Wei   3.

variants.name: Ngai
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Kwok, Kwong, Ming, Kwok Kwan, Wai, Yuet Ming, Cheung, Chi, Chi Kin, Chin, Chun, Fong.  Southeast Asian Hung, Chau, Dai, Tuan, Tung.
Chinese:
Cantonese form of the surnames 魏 and 危, see   Wei   1 and 2.

variants.name: Nguy
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Hoa, Minh, Quang, Thanh, Hao, Hue, Phat, Theng, Thuy, Tinh, Vinh, Anh, Au, Thai, Thay, Tuong.
Vietnamese (Ngụy): from the Chinese surname 魏, see Wei 1.
Vietnamese (Nguỳ): from the Chinese surname 危, see Wei 2.

variants.name: Wai
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Chi, Chan, Ming, Chi Kwong, Chien, Chow, Fong, Fung Ying, Hoi, Kam, Khin, Kin, Tha, Tin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 韋 and 衛, see   Wei   3 and 4.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 惠, see   Hui   1.

variants.name: Way
meanings: English (mainly southern): topographic name for someone who lived near a road or path, from Middle English wei (Old English weg, cognate with Old Norse vegr, Old High German weg), or a habitational name from some minor place called with this word, as for example any of the places called Way or Waye in Devon or Way in Somerset and Kent.
Chinese: variant of   Wei  .

variants.name: We
meanings: Chinese: variant of   Wei  .

nameMeanings.name: Marotte
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphonse, Marcel, Emile, Normand.
French: from a pet form of the female personal name   Marie  .
Altered form of French Marot, a variant of 1, reflecting the Canadian and American French practice of sounding the final -t.

variants.name: Marie
meanings: form of Maria . When first introduced to England in the Middle Ages, it was Anglicized in pronunciation and respelled Mary . This French form was reintroduced into the English-speaking world as a separate name in the 19th century, and is still pronounced more or less in the French manner, although sometimes with the stress on the first syllable. It is now often used in combination with other names such as Ellie, Chloe, and Lisa.

en.lifeSummary: Marotte de La Wez was born in 1190, in Wavrin, Nord, Hauts-de-France, France. She had at least 1 son with Baudouin II Robert de Wavrin Chevalier, Seigneur, de Wavrin. She died in 1234, at the age of 44.

fr.lifeSummary: Marotte de La Wez est née en 1190, à Wavrin, Nord, Hauts-de-France, France. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Baudouin II Robert de Wavrin Chevalier, Seigneur, de Wavrin. Elle est décédée en 1234, à l’âge de 44 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Marotte de La Wez wurde 1190 in Wavrin, Nord, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich geboren. Sie hatte mit Baudouin II Robert de Wavrin Chevalier, Seigneur, de Wavrin mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1234 im Alter von 44 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Marotte de La Wez è nata il nel 1190 nel Wavrin, Nord, Hauts-de-France, Francia. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Baudouin II Robert de Wavrin Chevalier, Seigneur, de Wavrin. Morì nel 1234, all'età di 44 anni.
